worthClicking.author: @ClaudeDevs
worthClicking.why: Official launch of /ultrareview — a fleet of cloud bug-hunting agents inside Claude Code, free for Pro/Max through 5/5; this is a meaningful product expansion with direct implications for how teams gate critical merges.

worthClicking.author: @levie
worthClicking.why: High-signal enterprise reality check: legacy tech stacks are the actual blocker to agent adoption, not model capability — useful framing for sales conversations about where implementation friction lives.

worthClicking.author: @aakashgupta
worthClicking.why: Karpathy's 1B-parameter / 1.8T-parameter compression claim reframed with supporting math — relevant to anyone making bets on model cost curves and what 'good enough' looks like in 18 months.

worthClicking.author: @emollick
worthClicking.why: Non-obvious finding: the LLM you pair with GPT-imagegen-2 materially changes output quality, especially for complex prompts — useful for anyone building image-generation features on top of the API.

worthClicking.author: @signulll
worthClicking.why: Tight observation that Anthropic's 0→$350B trajectory means employee #200 is getting equity outcomes historically reserved for employee #20 at Google — sharp framing of why talent is flowing there.

worthClicking.author: @emollick
worthClicking.why: Systems regulated by human effort cost (LoRs, lawsuits, essays) will structurally break under AI — compact, high-leverage observation with real product and sales implications.

worthClicking.author: @aakashgupta
worthClicking.why: Concrete data point: OpenAI Codex PMs now ship PRs instead of PRDs, using working code as the spec — directly relevant to the agent-native PM role thesis the operator has been tracking.

worthClicking.author: @jeff_weinstein
worthClicking.why: Stripe PM asking what signals would let webmasters welcome 'good' agents rather than block them — early framing of an agent identity/trust infrastructure problem that will matter for any product with an agent-facing surface.

voices.why: Produces grounded, empirically-supported observations about AI product behavior that aren't obvious — the GPT-imagegen-2 LLM-dependency finding is a good example.
Stripe-adjacent, thinking carefully about agent payments and agent identity infrastructure — early but directionally important for economic agents thread.
Low volume, high compression — punchy takes on AI company dynamics that cut through the hype.

examples: @aakashgupta engagement-farming off-topic threads (trees, In-N-Out, MJ estate, Xbox, NASA, creatine)
@DataChaz promotional tool-shill threads with near-zero engagement and no operator relevance
@RoundtableSpace all-caps headline screaming with crypto/prediction market filler

fading.theme: model competition (GPT-5.5 imminent, xAI voice APIs)
